blocked.evidence: 2026-07-20 historical evidence: the installed Codex Plugin contract has no supported declarative user-action or local browser-login registration surface. This remains valid only for custom Plugin UI and MCP designs. It does not block the existing host-triggered browser OAuth flow, which starts only from an already authorized API operation and does not require a Plugin UI action. See compatibility/capability-manifests/yeeflow-oauth-browser-login-entrypoint-platform-support.v1.0.0.json.

2026-07-18 historical blocker: TypeScript AST re-audit found parseDataViewFixedFilterConditionPart calls crypto.randomUUID for every materialized fixed-filter condition key, while buildDataListViewLayoutViewChecked mutates the caller-owned findings array. Phase 5H/5I subsequently established and shadow-proved an explicit host-owned filter-key and findings-lowering contract; Phase 5J accepted a future bounded Core shadow without reopening this historical production-routing block.

blocked.reason: No available export, Legacy, or product-runtime evidence proves a lossless stable relationship among parent ListID, parent FieldID, form/resource, summary, temporary-variable inventory, and temporary-variable identity. Names and mutable resource traversal are insufficient.
blocked.note: The original Legacy-only conclusion remains historical evidence. The new read-only Data List export proves configuration containment and binding relationships, not runtime lifecycle behavior.

blocked.evidence: 2026-07-19: embedded Sublist Lookup child target declaration, resolved target ListID propagation, and Legacy parity shape are absent from the current model; Phase 6 top-level resolution cannot be widened by inference.

blocked.reason: The authoritative static export still does not prove Data List Lookup selection-event timing, retrieval, clear-selection, invalid-target, destination mutation, or runtime read-only behavior. The configuration-only materializer route is now separately proven and does not claim those behaviors.
Provide Data List runtime/designer evidence covering selection, initialization, edit, repeat, clear, missing target, destination mutation, and readonly enforcement before any product runtime work.
No authoritative Data List export proves an un-routed embedded special-control configuration with an immutable deterministic boundary.
blocked.smallestPrerequisite: A read-only Data List export containing the exact embedded special-control configuration

blocked.reason: The authoritative Approval Form export proves a narrow Sublist Lookup target/display configuration, but the current Approval Form Legacy lowerer does not emit its lossless target/display keys. The other audited surfaces lack a matching authoritative export or an isolated immutable boundary. A Data List route cannot be reused because the two products have separate models and identities.
blocked.smallestPrerequisite: Establish, without changing production routing, an Approval Form-specific Legacy parity contract for embedded Sublist Lookup target/display metadata that preserves listid, appid, listsetid, and listfield as lossless configuration values.

blocked.reason: All four required Lookup target/display keys are either intentionally discarded before Approval Form materialization or exist only in imported/exported product data. No exact Legacy-generated representation exists.
blocked.smallestPrerequisite: A separately authorized Approval Form product-behavior enhancement with product/export/runtime evidence.

nextSteps.description: Create a checksummed isolated 1.0.0-rc.1 candidate workspace, build only there, and execute the declared source/archive/installed and application E2E matrix before any signing or isolated-install request.
Start Wave 3 Batch B only after this AST caller graph is used for its scoped caller, parity, distribution, and rollback proof.
Continue the calibrated Wave 3 registry with caller evidence, parity, distribution proof, and rollback.
Continue the calibrated Wave 3 registry with caller evidence, parity, distribution proof, and rollback.
Promote only the accepted immutable Core intent and non-mutating Local Runtime metadata APIs through the official builder, with four-surface and rollback proof.
Add adapter and routing proof only for the approved additional Type 0 Data List LayoutView boundary after separate authorization.
Use the versioned decomposition ledger to split subsequent mixed modules only after each selected vertical slice has Legacy/Core differential evidence.
Run focused tests and record results without commit, PR, release, or stable promotion.

worktreeProtection.policy: Preserve all listed paths. Do not delete, stage, rename, or include protected duplicate files in migration scope.

realtimeBaseline.head: fcc8b8e16b784f1032759ee1b6b588a144497feb
realtimeBaseline.sourceDistParityEvidence: The versioned topology contract classifies every source and dist script as exact mirror, transformed mirror, root compatibility copy, source-only development tool, or generated distribution artifact. SHA-256 verification passed for exact mirrors and no unknown topology or semantic-classification finding remains.

englishOnlyGate.scope: migration-owned changed files by default; explicit path, Git changed file, and generated artifact directory inputs are supported

materializerSeamAudit.analysisMethod: TypeScript AST top-level function inventory, local call graph, direct helper module scan, and conservative host-effect propagation.

normalizeHexColorShadow.rollback: Temporary full Plugin copy removes the adapter import and replaces coreNormalizeHexColor with normalizeHexColor at the four scoped call expressions.
